ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is the closest parent; no specific entry exists for quinoa-tofu plant-based burger with satay sauce.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (burger, meat substitute) is the closest defensible parent category for a tofu-quinoa patty burger; no exact code for quinoa-tofu satay variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific plant-based burger preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One quinoa tofu patty burger with satay sauce, approximately 280 g, served as a complete meal at a restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Quinoa cooked (FDC ID 168917), Firm tofu (FDC ID 172475), Hamburger bun (FDC ID 174924), Peanut satay sauce (FDC ID 172430 analogous), and standard burger assembly proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for quinoa tofu patty burger with satay sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values estimated by weighted component analysis: quinoa cooked ~25%, firm tofu ~20%, burger bun ~25%, satay peanut sauce ~10%, vegetables (lettuce, tomato, onion) ~20%. Values are per 100 g of the assembled burger. Energy cross-checked using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed plant-based burger estimated from components: quinoa (GI ~53, Atkinson et al. 2008), tofu (GI ~15, University of Sydney GI Database), white bread/bun (GI ~75), peanut sauce (GI ~14 analogous to peanuts); weighted composite GI ~48
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. Composite GI estimated by weighting component GI values by carbohydrate contribution: quinoa cooked GI 53 (Atkinson et al. 2008, University of Sydney GI Database), firm tofu GI 15 (University of Sydney GI Database), hamburger bun (white bread analogue) GI 75 (University of Sydney GI Database), peanut satay sauce GI 14 (peanut analogue, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI approximately 48, classified as low (<=55). Glycemic load per serving = (48 x 68.6 g available carbohydrate per 280 g serving) / 100 = 32.9, rounded to 32.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~868 mg per serving) from satay sauce and burger bun
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that one serving provides approximately 868 mg sodium (~37% of the 2300 mg daily limit); requesting reduced satay sauce or a lower-sodium bun can meaningfully reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~32.7 per serving) despite low GI (48), driven by the burger bun carbohydrate volume
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the composite GI is low (48), the total glycemic load per serving is moderate (~32.7); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider consuming only half the bun or opting for a lettuce wrap to reduce postprandial glucose excursion, while benefiting from the protein and fibre from quinoa and tofu.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.4 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, and beneficial polyunsaturated fats from peanut satay sauce and tofu
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This plant-based burger is relatively heart-friendly due to its low saturated fat content, zero cholesterol, and inclusion of polyunsaturated fats; however, the moderate sodium level warrants attention for individuals with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Quinoa Tofu Patty Burger with Satay Sauce is a plant-based fusion burger served at Flavours by Saute in Singapore. The patty is hand-made from cooked quinoa and firm tofu, providing a protein-rich, cholesterol-free alternative to conventional meat patties. The burger is assembled with a standard bun, fresh vegetables (lettuce, tomato, onion), and finished with a peanut-based satay sauce that imparts a distinctly Southeast Asian flavour profile — nutty, mildly sweet, and slightly spiced. The texture of the patty is firm yet tender, with the quinoa contributing a subtle crunch and the tofu providing a soft, cohesive bind. Nutritionally, the burger delivers approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with 8.2 g protein, 7.8 g fat (predominantly unsaturated), and 24.5 g carbohydrate. Dietary fibre is 2.8 g/100 g, contributed by quinoa and vegetables. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), making it a more glycaemically moderate choice compared to conventional beef burgers on white buns. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, is dairy-free, and is likely halal. Key allergens include gluten (bun), soy (tofu), and peanuts (satay sauce). Clinically, the moderate sodium content from the satay sauce (~310 mg/100 g) is the primary concern for hypertensive individuals, while the low GI and plant-based protein profile make it a reasonable option for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk, provided portion size is monitored.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for this specific dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked quinoa, firm tofu, hamburger bun, peanut sauce, and fresh vegetables. GI is a composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated (USDA FoodData Central); no direct laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this dish. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant burger of this type. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero by ingredient composition; this is a plant-based patty with no hydrogenated fats. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as all ingredients are plant-derived (quinoa, tofu, vegetables, peanut sauce). 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients or fortification reported. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; no animal-derived or fortified ingredients present. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not -1 sentinel values. 7. GI composite estimate: quinoa GI 53, tofu GI 15, white bun GI 75, peanut sauce GI 14; weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component. 8. The input food_group 'Oils, seasonings and condiments' has been corrected to 'Mixed' as this is a complete burger meal, not a condiment. 9. Dietary flag is_vegan set to true based on ingredient composition; restaurant should be consulted to confirm no animal-derived additives in the satay sauce. 10. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA FoodData Central component analysis; no -1 values remain in per_100g. 11. Q8 fix (iteration 3): aliases consistency audit performed — every string in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, romanized) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. The entry 'Quinoa Doufu Hanbao Pei Shadiejiang' from aliases_by_language.romanized was added to the flat aliases array to ensure complete consistency.
